item.title: The Idiots of Imgur: A Year-End Dump 2024 Edition
item.description:
Thanks to a suggestion from @agreedydemon I made a dump consisting of this year's best content! I took the top 5 posts with the most upvotes and then ordered the content within each post based on how many upvotes it got in the comments from lowest to highest. I started counting these about a week ago, so some of the numbers may have shifted around, but I think it gets the job done. Thanks to everyone who tags me when they see good stuff in the wild and I hope everyone who sees this has an amazing 2025! We're starting this off with #5: Mini dump 191 which got 2169 upvotes at the time of counting.
This video had the most reaction in the comments. I counted 1222 references to this one.
Moving onto #4: Mini dump 144 which got 2190 upvotes.
Onto #3: Mini dump 182 2195 We're all pretty sure this one is from a movie, right? Anyone know what one exactly? Put it at the bottom of this dump's rank since it probably shouldn't be here.
In the #2 spot we have: Mini dump 159 with 2265 votes
And finally we have the dump with the most upvotes for 2024: Mini dump 147 with 2496 upvotes! I hope you all enjoyed the post, and hope you all have a fun and safe New Years!
item.dc:creator: UncleSlam123

item.title: Most Interesting Things Seen on Structural Assessments in 2024!
item.description:
Does anyone want the plumber's special? It involves removing all structural support for your home. Plumbers broke through this foundation to add pipes, compromising the structural support of this home.
This crawlspace had no proper ventilation, so when water seeped into it, it had nowhere to go and air out. This left the water to sit and soak into the wood, leading to severe mold and wood rot that spread throughout the crawlspace.
The heavy rains this year triggered a series of landslides in our area, prompting numerous calls for assistance. The sudden downpour had immediate and devastating consequences, as seen in this garage filled with over 10 feet of mud.
Unfortunately, this landslide crashed through this house. Mud engulfed the walkway and cascaded down the stairs, causing significant damage and posing a serious threat to the safety of the residents.
Another landslide that caused significant damage to this home.
The force of the landslide destroyed a back wall, allowing mud to find its way into almost every room. Including the bathroom… yikes. This is going to be a massive cleanup effort.
Just wait for it… All this questionable extra framing is unnecessary and, to top it off, it isn't even sitting on the pier. If you looked up "Mickey Mouse Construction," this video would come up as the definition.
The neighbors removed their retaining wall without realizing it was providing support for this other wall, ultimately leading to its failure.
Movement in Rancho Palos Verdes continues to take its toll. This 6-foot drop has eliminated direct access to the front of this home.
Here is a different home ripping apart due to that same land movement.
The house is splitting down the middle as the shifting ground beneath it destabilizes the foundation.
What was once a slow movement of about an inch a year has now turned catastrophic for some residents. The City of RPV estimates the land is moving at a rate of about four to five feet per month.
This homeowner uses a rope to walk up the driveway and into their home. Now, with the recent shift, even that might be too difficult.
This parking garage is dealing with an extremely severe case of spall damage, and unfortunately this isn’t the only area affected. The pool, spa, and planters above have all been leaking, contributing to the extensive deterioration throughout the structure.
This crawlspace was built just four years ago, and it’s already dealing with severe mold issues, which is a fast track to rot, decay, and costly repairs. Over time, the mold has been rotting out the floor joists and beams, compromising the entire structure.
Check out what one of our structural assessors, Alfie, had to say about the state of the crawlspace.
A huge landslide crashed into this home and caused extensive damage.
Heavy rains can trigger landslides, especially in hillside areas, as the soil becomes oversaturated and loses stability. It's been a devastating situation for the homeowner, highlighting the powerful and unpredictable nature of slope movement.
Well, this is just upsetting. Other contractors that were called to retrofit this home didn’t even bother bolting the URFP to the foundation—it’s just sitting there, doing nothing. It’s frustrating to think this homeowner believed their home was protected from seismic activity, only for us to discover that the work wasn’t done properly in the first place.
This house is being torn apart by land movement. We've visited before, and it's alarming to see how much has shifted in just a few short months.
Due to last winter's heavy rains, this retaining wall fell over and down the slope. This is a prime example of why proper construction and maintenance are critical, as a poorly built wall can fail at any given moment. We see a lot of these failures, especially when the rainy season hits, which is why being proactive is so important.
Severe spall damage has left this foundation weak and crumbling. Now, it’s gotten to the point where sections can be easily pulled apart by hand. The spall damage has corroded the rebar, and without intervention, it will only continue spreading and weakening other areas. A foundation is supposed to be supporting your home, but it’s hard to sleep easy knowing this is what’s beneath you.
It looks like The Very Hungry Caterpillar was nearby. Water is leaking from a planter above this area which is now causing issues. The water intrusion has led to severe corrosion and has completely eaten away at the steel.
Watch your step... Wood rot completely took out this set of stairs.
Wingardium leviosa… did they hire a contractor from Knockturn Alley? Another contractor installed concrete piers hanging from the floor joists of this property. If this was their attempt at a post-and-pier foundation, they're a long way off from doing it right.
When the unlicensed Contractor needs to make the best of a 14-foot-tall retaining wall falling down. NO, this wall was not built this way. YES, this wall is 14 feet tall. Below the hilarious statue the slab is sitting on is a subterranean garage with 10 foot+ tall ceiling height. Thank you for joining us, we hope you enjoyed! Have a Happy New Year, and check out our Instagram for more exciting content. We'll see you next Tuesday at 2 PM! https://www.instagram.com/alphastructural/
item.dc:creator: AlphaStructural

item.title: Living by yourself can be lonely
item.dc:creator: SkyPigeon123

item.title: Balancing fisherman tutorial
item.description:
Made a few of these for fishing friends. You could adapt to other designs.
4d 1-1/4” finish nails. 8d 2-1/2” framing nail. 1/4” round stock, 1/8x1/2 flat bar - 1” long. Not pictured, 11” length of wire and 1-1/2” long piece of flat bar to make the fish.
Cut two of the finish nails to 1” length. Cut the framing nail head and point off.
Bend the 1” cut nails as shown. Layout against the flat bar. I put them both in a vise and hammer them over at the same time so they’re consistent.
Weld the “hands” together.
Put a small bend in the elbows.
Set the arms at the angle you like and weld to body.
Set the legs and weld into place.
For the head, round it as desired before cutting. Easier to hold that way.
Cut it off to whatever length you like.
I welded a bead on the bottom of the head to make a neck. You don’t have to.
Maybe shoulda welded the head before the arms. I welded from the back.
Weld the fishing rod as shown. Don’t worry too much about the angle. Everything can be bent to adjust later.
Cut a piece of wire about 11” long. Bend a curve in it around a 4” radius.
Weld the wire to the rod at about a right angle.
I cut the fish out of the same flat bar, 1-1/2” long. After cutting I polished it up with a flapper wheel. I used a sanding disc on the dremel to give the sorta fish scale design.
Lay it flat and weld the fish to the wire.
I heated it with a torch to get a nice blue/purple oxide. While it’s hot, I apply some oil to help prevent rust.
More blue oxide.
item.dc:creator: bloomautomatic

item.title: By Your Smallest Acts of Kindness...
item.dc:creator: FrostyLady

item.title: Backlogged memes: these colors don’t run
item.description:
Hello all, I hope the new year treats you well because wtf was going on with 2024 Best year of my life in many ways don’t get me wrong. Pregnant with kiddo #2, got a publishing deal for my book baby, moved to Germany, made some good friends. But globally speaking. WOOF.
Yeah they do. That’s not why you work on yourself lol. It’s so you can find non toxic people who like you and not drive them away.
Oh man that would be absolutely baller. Something pre pandemic please.
Reading this lowkey changed my life, man.
Grade A pun I don’t have a lot to say today except a PSA. if you were one of the many many people who commented on my previous post about how easy it is for you to set the time on your ovens, how’s the view from the top of mount privilege? Seriously. When we moved here we bought the oven from the previous tenant and my engineer husband searched for hours online before he could figure it out. Turns out there are four buttons and you have to press #2 and #4 simultaneously and twist (not press) #1 to set the time. Otherwise the oven will refuse to turn on at all. The instructions video was in German. If anyone ever has trouble with setting clocks do not mock them.
Deep.
The daycare was open for literally one day over the past three weeks. This was the vibe when we dropped him off.
At this point someone just put me down, man. I can’t have anything I enjoy while pregnant. No antidepressants, no alcohol, no junk food (everything I’m craving is difficult to get here, German junk food just ain’t cutting it). No ibuprofen. German Tylenol is pretty ineffective. This sucks.
I know which I’d rather be.
This episode shit on guinea pigs so hard you guys it was actually hilarious
I actually do think I lost faith in humanity. I found this lost cat and I put up an ad on the German version of Nextdoor and literally the only two commenters were just asking why the ad was so weirdly phrased. They are not the first to make fun of my German no matter how hard I am trying. I gave up after that. That and The Betrayal but I’m not supposed to talk about that. Oh well who cares.
Oh it’s gonna be.
Oh. Oh lord.
My husband has been picking up some of the slack thank god. But like. Why do kids have to make it so difficult all the time???
Yeah I’d kill him too. My friends divorcing her husband currently bc he pulled shit like this.
It really does help
I do not know this phrase but I must
When she doesn’t have to ask for help at Lowe’s…. 20x hotter. Both are good.
Really hope this turns out, idk why it’s like this
I wish
Probably still toxic but I dig it
What does that phrase mean. Is it racist.
Cat tax. Rudy is watching. HNY everyone!
item.dc:creator: kirbyvictorious

item.title: this is my dog on drugs
item.description: Our old man usually gets a little trazodone on fireworks holidays, to help him deal. He's kind of a lightweight and 50mg will zonk him out. He's also started to experience some sundowning recently - probably the start of some age-related cognitive dysfunction. So he might start getting a little evening treat to take some of the edge off.
item.dc:creator: TheChunguskaEvent
